references.text: Eriksson, R. 2007. A synopsis of Basellaceae. Kew Bulletin 62: 297-320.
Steenis, CGGJ van. 1955. Basellaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 5: 300-304

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 21 October 2017

species.citation: (Lam.) C.F.Gaertn. , Suppl. Carp. 3 (1807) 176; ≡ Basella vesicaria Lam. , Tabl. Encycl. 1 (1792) 382. = Anredera scandens Sm. , Cycl. 39 (1818) n.1 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 201; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 137; Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1957) 304.
species.distribution: America. LUZON: Batangas, Cavite, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Rizal.
species.notes: Introduced to the Philippines by the Spaniards and thoroughly naturalized by the early 19th century. Common in thickets at low and medium elevation, preferring areas with pronounced dry season. Does not produce seeds locally.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 272 ; Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1957) 301. = Basella rubra L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 272 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 200; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 137.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Native country not exactly known but probably native in SE Asia. Throughout the Philippines in settled areas, mostly in the lowlands, often cultivated. Common name: alugbati. 0-500m.
